title: M.Sc. Public Health Administration
content: The M.Sc. in Public Health Administration is a postgraduate science programme designed to provide in-depth knowledge and analytical skills in public health sciences and health systems administration. The programme integrates epidemiology, biostatistics, environmental and occupational health, public health nutrition, health policy, and management sciences to prepare graduates for scientific, administrative, and research-oriented roles in the health sector. The curriculum emphasizes quantitative analysis, research methodology, systems thinking, and evidence-based public health practice, supported by fieldwork, practical training, and a mandatory dissertation. Graduates of the programme are equipped to contribute effectively to public health research, programme implementation, health administration, and policy development at regional, national, and global levels. Bachelor Degree in Basic Health or Allied Science with minimum 50% marks or equivalent. Students with a CUET score will be given preference in the merit list. From an industry perspective, there is a growing demand for professionals who can combine public health knowledge with administrative and managerial skills. Graduates can work in: 01 The M.Sc. in Public Health Administration program aims to develop skilled public health professionals equipped with knowledge and managerial competencies to plan, implement, and evaluate healthcare programs and policies. 02 The program focuses on strengthening leadership, health system management, epidemiological understanding, health promotion strategies, and evidence-based decision-making to address emerging public health challenges and improve community health outcomes at local, national, and global levels. 01 Demonstrate advanced knowledge of public health sciences, health systems, and administrative frameworks. 02 Apply epidemiological and biostatistical techniques for data analysis and evidence-based decision-making. 03 Plan, manage, and evaluate public health programmes and health services. 04 Assess environmental, occupational, nutritional, and disease-related health risks using scientific approaches. 05 Communicate public health findings effectively through written, oral, and digital formats. 06 Demonstrate leadership, teamwork, and ethical responsibility in public health practice and administration. 07 Employ research and critical thinking skills to address complex public health challenges. 08 Pursue lifelong learning, higher education, or professional roles in public health and health administration. 01 Apply scientific principles of public health and health administration to analyze and address population health problems. 02 Design, implement, and evaluate public health programmes using epidemiological, biostatistical, and systems-based approaches. 03 Demonstrate administrative and leadership competencies in health systems, public health organizations, and allied sectors. 04 Engage in research, higher studies, and continuous professional development in public health and related disciplines. 01 Apply epidemiological and biostatistical methods to investigate public health issues and interpret health data. 02 Analyze health systems, policies, and administrative structures using quantitative and qualitative tools. 03 Evaluate environmental, occupational, nutritional, and communicable disease determinants of health. 04 Use research methodologies, health databases, and digital tools for public health analysis and decision-making. 05 Conduct independent research and prepare scientific reports, dissertations, and policy briefs in public health.
